document.title: Analysis of Image Quality in Digital Chest Imaging
productModels: InSight Thoracic imaging film and screen

sourceSummary.oneLine: Journal article evaluating image quality of amorphous silicon flat-panel detector, computed radiography, and screen-film chest imaging systems using contrast-detail phantom images.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Literature evidence included under clinical validation and verification materials for a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector technical document.
keyPoints: The study compared an amorphous silicon flat-panel detector system, a computed radiology system, and a screen-film system using contrast-detail phantom images.
The amorphous silicon flat-panel detector system showed significantly better low-contrast visibility than the screen-film system while operating at reduced patient dose in clinical settings.
The computed radiology system showed low-contrast visibility comparable to the screen-film system.
Soft copy reading at full resolution with contrast and brightness adaptation produced the best results.
Tube voltage from 102 to 133 kV and additional filtration did not significantly affect image quality, while increasing exposure improved low-contrast visibility.

concepts.name: Digital chest imaging
concepts.evidence: ANALYSIS OF IMAGE QUALITY IN DIGITAL CHEST IMAGING

concepts.name: Contrast-detail phantom image quality evaluation
concepts.evidence: low-contrast resolution was assessed using a contrast-detail phantom

concepts.name: Computed radiography
concepts.evidence: The computed radiography (CR) system used in the study

concepts.name: Low-contrast visibility
concepts.evidence: The higher the IQF inv, the better is the low-contrast visibility.

evidence.claim: The article evaluated image quality of amorphous silicon flat-panel detector and computed radiology systems compared with a screen-film system.
evidence.evidenceText: An evaluation of the image quality of an amorphous silicon flat-panel detector system and a computed radiology system compared with a screen-film system was performed by means of contrast-detail phantom images.

evidence.claim: The flat-panel detector system used in the study was Vertix FD from Siemens.
evidence.evidenceText: The amorphous silicon flat-panel detector system (FP) used in this study (Vertix FD; Siemens, Germany)

evidence.claim: The flat-panel detector used a Trixell pixium 4600 amorphous silicon image detector with a 3000 x 3000 pixel matrix.
evidence.evidenceText: The 43 x 43 cm amorphous silicon image detector (Trixell pixium 4600, France) has a 3000 x 3000 pixel matrix

evidence.claim: The computed radiography system used GP DirectView CR cassettes and a DirectView CR 900 read-out unit from Eastman Kodak.
evidence.evidenceText: GP DirectView CR cassettes; Eastman Kodak, USA... read-out unit (DirectView CR 900; Eastman Kodak)

evidence.claim: The study used the CDRAD 2.0 contrast-detail phantom to evaluate image quality.
evidence.evidenceText: For evaluation of the image quality taking into account both spatial and contrast resolution, the CDRAD 2.0 contrast-detail phantom... was used.

evidence.claim: For standard PA exposure, contrast-detail curves of the flat-panel system were significantly better than screen-film for all displaying modalities.
evidence.evidenceText: For a standard PA-exposure, contrast-detail curves of the FP-system were significantly better than contrast-detail curves of the SF-system for all displaying modalities.

evidence.claim: The flat-panel system performed significantly better than the computed radiography system.
evidence.evidenceText: The FP-system also performed significantly better than the CR-system.

evidence.claim: No statistical difference was found between computed radiography and screen-film contrast-detail curves.
evidence.evidenceText: No statistical difference was found between the contrast-detail curves of the CR-system and the SF-system.

evidence.claim: A prior dosimetry study on the same systems showed the flat-panel system operated at about 40% of the screen-film dose and computed radiography at about 65%.
evidence.evidenceText: a dosimetry study on these systems showed that the FP-system operated at ~40% of the dose of the SF-system, and the CR-system at ~65% of that dose.

evidence.claim: Soft copy reading at full resolution with contrast and brightness adaptation produced better contrast-detail curves for both digital systems.
evidence.evidenceText: For both digital systems, SC(+) readings lead to significantly better contrast-detail curves than both SC(-) and HC readings

evidence.claim: Tube voltage from 102 to 133 kV and additional filtration from 0.0 to 0.3 mm Cu did not significantly affect image quality.
evidence.evidenceText: the tube voltage (102-133 kV) or additional filtration (0.0-0.3 mm Cu). However, low-contrast visibility significantly improved with increasing exposure

evidence.claim: The conclusion states full field digital radiography systems based on amorphous silicon flat-panel detector technology are a breakthrough in chest imaging.
evidence.evidenceText: Full Field Digital Radiography systems, based on amorphous silicon flat-panel detector technology, are a breakthrough in chest imaging.

evidence.claim: The article states patient doses can be lowered in practice when using full field digital radiography systems.
evidence.evidenceText: When using the same dose as with a corresponding film-screen system, image quality is significantly better. In practice, patient doses can therefore be lowered.
